solution.p: First we square each side to obtain a quadratic equation. Then we can rearrange that into standard form.
Now we can identify a={1}\text{,} b={3}\text{,} and c={2}\text{,} and apply the quadratic formula.
The only possible solutions are -2 and -1\text{.} They each check as valid solutions in the original equation, so the solution set is {\left\{-2,-1\right\}}\text{.}
